Fixed-target effluent PO4 from SR, calculated from corrected influent PO4 to SR (65.49 mg/L, mass-balance-corrected per urine diversion split using f_urine_P=0.61 applied to total P mass then divided by urine-only flow volume). Expected: 90% P removal (Etter et al. 2011, Water Res. 45, 852-862; Maurer et al. 2006). Low: 98% P removal (Antonini et al. 2011, CLEAN-Soil Air Water, validated on diluted urine, most relevant to this system's dilute influent). High: 58% P removal (EnviroLoo field reactor, IC_P_Recovery_Overview_Illinois_University_Updated, real-world non-optimized performance).

Fraction of precipitated struvite captured as solid (harvest efficiency), distinct from eff_PO4_mgL (P removal from solution). Expected: 0.70, EnviroLoo field reactor trial harvest efficiency of 68.7% (IC_P_Recovery_Overview_Illinois_University_Updated). Low: 0.55, consistent with pilot-scale struvite harvest losses from crystal washout (Hug, A., Udert, K.M. 2018-style pilot studies; Antonini et al. 2011 pilot reported 94% precipitation but only 55% recovery due to crystal washout). High: 0.85, reflects potential for improved harvest under more controlled conditions than the field trial, while remaining below idealized lab values.
